quick_skim.title: The Thinking Pixel: Recursive Sparse Reasoning in Multimodal Diffusion Latents
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion models have achieved success in high-fidelity data synthesis, yet their capacity for more complex, structured reasoning like text following tasks remains constrained. While advances in language models have leveraged strategies such as latent reasoning and recursion to enhance text understanding capabilities, extending these to multimodal text-to-image generation tasks is challenging due to the continuous and non-discrete nature of visual tokens. To tackle this problem, we draw inspiration from modular human cognition and propose a recursive, sparse mixture-of-experts framework integrated into conventional diffusion models. Our approach introduces a recursive component within joint attention layers that iteratively refines visual tokens over multiple latent steps while efficiently sharing parameters via sparse selection of neural modules. At each step, a gating network is devised to dynamically select specialized neural modules, conditioned on the current visual tokens, the diffusion timestep, and the conditioning information. Comprehensive evaluation on class-conditioned ImageNet image generation tasks and additional studies on the GenEval and DPG benchmark demonstrate the superiority of the proposed method in enhancing model image generation performance.

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a recursive sparse MoE framework to enhance structured reasoning in multimodal diffusion models.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 引入递归稀疏混合专家框架，通过迭代细化视觉标记来增强多模态扩散模型的结构化推理能力。

quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 帮我检索自回归扩散模型的研究进展, especially work that connects or combines: autoregressive diffusion models research progress; AR-Diffusion model architecture and performance; autoregressive sequence generation with diffusion; combining autoregressive models and diffusion models; recent advances in autoregressive diffusion models; state of the art autoregressive diffusion architectures.
